exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Feet planted, slight arch, shoulder blades pinched back and down
Lower dumbbells to chest level, elbows about 45° from torso
Press up and slightly together without clanking the bells
Control the descent — 2 seconds down

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Bench at 30–45° — higher hits shoulders more than chest
Dumbbells start at upper-chest level, elbows about 45° from torso
Press up and slightly together over the collarbones
Lower under control to a full stretch, no bouncing off the chest

exercises.name: Dumbbell Chest Fly
instructions: Flat bench, dumbbells over the chest, palms facing each other
Soft bend in the elbows and hold it — this is a fly, not a press
Open the arms in a wide arc until you feel the chest stretch
Squeeze back up along the same arc; go lighter than you'd press

exercises.name: Overhead Press
instructions: Start with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward
Brace abs, don't flare ribs — press straight up
Finish with biceps by ears, don't bang the bells
Lower under control to ear level

exercises.name: Lateral Raises
instructions: Slight bend in elbows, lead with the elbows not the hands
Raise out to the sides to shoulder height, no higher
Tip the pinky slightly up at the top, like pouring water
Lower slow — no swinging; go lighter if you're shrugging

exercises.name: Lying Triceps Extensions
instructions: Lie back, dumbbells pressed up over chest, palms facing each other
Bend only at the elbows — lower the bells beside your ears
Upper arms stay vertical and still the whole time
Extend back up without flaring elbows out

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Cable at the top, chest out, shoulder blades back
Elbows pinned to your sides — they're the only joint that moves
Push down to full lockout, squeeze the triceps a beat
Control the bar back up to chest height, no shoulder roll

exercises.name: Dips
instructions: Hands on parallel bars, arms locked out, shoulders pulled down away from your ears
Lean the torso forward slightly to bias the chest — stay upright for more triceps
Lower under control until the shoulders drop just below the elbows, feeling the stretch
Press back to a strong lockout without shrugging; no bouncing out of the bottom

exercises.name: Assisted Hangs / Progression
instructions: Full grip on the bar, hands about shoulder width
Keep shoulders active — pull them slightly down away from ears
Use feet on a box/band only as much as needed, arms stay straight
Build time under tension — count the seconds

exercises.name: Scap Pull-Ups
instructions: Start from a dead hang, arms completely straight
Without bending elbows, pull shoulder blades down and together
Chest lifts a few inches — pause 1 second at the top
Lower back to full hang under control

exercises.name: One-Arm Dumbbell Rows
instructions: Knee and hand on bench, back flat like a table
Pull dumbbell to hip, not to armpit
Elbow stays close to body, no torso twist
Full stretch at the bottom each rep

exercises.name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Upper back on the bench, feet planted, hips slightly below shoulders
Hold one dumbbell with both palms flat under the top plate, arms up over chest
Slight elbow bend held constant — lower the bell in an arc behind your head
Stop at a deep lat stretch, then pull back over the chest with the lats, not the arms

exercises.name: Bent-Over Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Hinge at the hips until your torso is near parallel to the floor
Dumbbells hang under the chest, palms facing each other, slight elbow bend
Raise the bells out to the sides, leading with the elbows — squeeze the rear delts
Keep it light and slow; don't swing or let the torso rise to cheat the weight up

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Bench at ~45°, sit back fully, arms hanging straight down
Curl without letting elbows drift forward
Squeeze at the top, lower slow to a full stretch
Shoulders stay pinned to the bench

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Brace upper arm on the incline bench (or your thigh), armpit snug against the pad
Curl up without lifting the elbow off the pad
Lower all the way to a full stretch — that's where it works
No swinging; the pad exists to stop cheating

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Grip just outside shoulder width, thighs locked under the pads
Slight lean back, chest up — pull the bar to the upper chest
Drive the elbows down and back, don't yank with the biceps
Slow stretch back to the top — full hang between reps

exercises.name: Face Pulls
instructions: Cable (or band) set at about head height, rope handle, palms facing in
Pull the rope toward your eyes, splitting the ends apart as your hands reach your face
Lead with high elbows — squeeze the rear delts and upper back at the end
Light weight, slow return; this is about control and the squeeze, not load

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold dumbbell vertically against chest, elbows down
Feet shoulder width, toes slightly out
Sit down between your heels — chest stays up
Elbows track inside knees at the bottom, drive up through mid-foot

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Soft knees — this is a hip hinge, not a squat
Push hips back, dumbbells slide down thighs
Stop when hamstrings pull tight (around mid-shin), back flat
Stand up by squeezing glutes, don't yank with lower back

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Upper back against the bench, feet flat, a dumbbell across the hips (pad it)
Drive through the heels and lift until hips are level with knees and shoulders
Squeeze the glutes hard at the top, ribs down — don't arch the lower back
Lower under control; chin tucked, shins vertical at the top

exercises.name: Reverse Lunges
instructions: Dumbbells at sides, step one foot straight back
Lower until both knees hit ~90°, torso tall
Front heel stays planted — drive through it to stand
All reps one leg, then switch; log reps per leg

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Top of rear foot on the bench, front foot far enough forward to stay balanced
Feet hip-width apart, not on a tightrope — dumbbells at your sides
Drop the back knee straight down, torso tall (lean forward slightly for more glute)
Drive up through the front heel; all reps one leg, then switch — log reps per leg

exercises.name: Calf Raises
instructions: Balls of feet on edge, heels hanging
Full stretch at the bottom — pause there
Drive up as high as possible, hold the top 1 second
Slow tempo beats heavy weight here

exercises.name: Leg Press
instructions: Feet shoulder width, mid-platform; back and hips stay glued to the pad
Lower until knees hit ~90° — don't let the hips curl off the seat
Press through mid-foot and heels, knees tracking over toes
Never lock the knees hard at the top — keep a soft finish

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
instructions: Face down, pad resting just above the heels, hips pressed into the bench
Curl the heels toward your glutes — squeeze the hamstrings hard at the top
Keep the hips down; don't let them pop up to swing the weight
Lower slow to a full stretch, no crashing the stack

exercises.name: Incline Walk / Walk
instructions: Zone 2 pace — you can hold a conversation, but singing would be hard
On a treadmill, 2.5–3.5 mph at 5–12% incline beats flat-and-fast
Tall posture, don't hang on the rails — swing the arms
Log minutes; distance is optional if you're outside or the display shows it

exercises.name: Run / Jog
instructions: Easy conversational pace for most runs — slow is the point
Short quick steps, feet landing under your hips, not out in front
Relaxed shoulders and hands; breathe through a rhythm you can keep
Build time before speed — add ~10% a week, no hero jumps

exercises.name: Jump Rope
instructions: Elbows pinned to your sides — the wrists spin the rope, not the arms
Jump barely an inch off the floor, land soft on the balls of the feet
Rope length: stand on the middle, handles reach about armpit height
Go in short rounds (30–60s) with rest between; log total minutes

exercises.name: Bike / Row / Machine
instructions: Zone 2 effort — steady, conversational, sweat without gasping
Rowing: legs push first, then lean, then pull — reverse on the way back
Bike: seat height so the knee stays slightly bent at the bottom
Log minutes; add distance if the console shows it

exercises.name: StairMaster
instructions: Fingertips on the rails for balance only — no leaning or death-grip
Full foot on each step, push through the heel, stand tall
Steady conversational pace beats a sprint you can't hold
Log minutes; distance can stay blank (the mi field doesn't apply)

exercises.name: Plank
instructions: Elbows under the shoulders, forearms flat, feet about hip-width
Squeeze the glutes and tuck the ribs down — no sagging hips, no piked butt
Head neutral, eyes at the floor a few inches ahead of your hands
Breathe. If your form breaks, the set is over — stop the clock and log it

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from the bar, shoulders pulled down and away from your ears
Curl the pelvis up first — think 'roll the hips', not 'lift the feet'
Lower slowly and kill the swing before the next rep
Too hard straight-legged? Bend the knees — that's the same exercise, easier

exercises.name: Russian Twist
instructions: Sit leaning back ~45°, chest tall — don't let the lower back round
Rotate from the ribcage, not the arms; the weight follows your chest
Slow and controlled beats fast and floppy — pause at each side
Count both sides as the total (left + right = 2). Feet down is fine

exercises.name: Dead Bug
instructions: On your back, arms straight up, knees over hips bent at 90°
Press the lower back flat into the floor and keep it there the whole set
Extend the opposite arm and leg slowly, only as far as the back stays flat
If the back arches, you went too far — shorten the range, don't rush
